subject: [DCAEGEN2] Deploy DCAE microservices via Helm
commit_message:
[DCAEGEN2] Deploy DCAE microservices via Helm

Deploy DCAE microservices using Helm instead of having
the DCAE bootstrap container deploy them using Cloudify.

Charts for the microservices are found under
oom/kubernetes/dcaegen2-services.

Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2615
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-2617
Signed-off-by: Jack Lucas <jflos@sonoris.net>
Change-Id: I22d88987ae8e21b3c08f31f13ffda98967d13297
[Use common secret template for secrets]
Signed-off-by: Krzysztof Opasiak <k.opasiak@samsung.com>

Patch Set 1:

This change introduces oom/kubernetes/dcaegen2-services, with Helm charts that deploy DCAE
collection and analytics microservices.  These were previously deployed by the DCAE bootstrap
container using Cloudify.

Because the DCAE microservices need very similar sets of Kubernetes resources, the bulk of 
the templating is done in a set of common templates in dcaegen2-services/common/dcaegen2-services-common.

Patch Set 1:

This change is ready for review.

Patch Set 5:

Patchset 4 verified properly.  The gating results look good, but it turns out that the gating process did not enable the new dcaegen2-services charts.   In the onap values.yaml, we added a flag for dcaegen2-services.enabled, and by default it is set to false.  The override file for the gating process needs to be changed to set the flag to true.

In patchset 5, I have submitted changes to oom/kubernetes/onap/resources/environments/*.yaml and
oom/kubernetes/onap/resources/overrides/*.yaml.  I've added an entry for dcaegen2-services everywhere there is an entry for dcaegen2, and I've set the "enabled" flag for dcaegen2-services to match the
setting for the dcaegen2 "enabled" flag.

If gating is using the files in onap/resources, then these changes should cause the new dcaegen2-services charts to be deployed.

Patch Set 5:

Patchset 5 uses the new charts to deploy hv-ves, prh, tcagen2, and ves as expected.  All four come up and become ready.  The separate "healthcheck" cases for hv-ves and ves succeed.  The pnfregistrate "smoketest" succeeds.  The 5gbulkpm fails, but I am not sure why.  (I'm not sure how dcae is involved
in the test.)
The dcaegen2 healthcheck fails.  This is because it is detecting a mongo instance being deployed dynamically by one of the other healthcheck/smoketests and it is present but not reporting as ready.  We observed this previously.

BulkPM failure looks like due to DMAAP-BC not available. As both DataFileCollector and PM-Mapper rely on secure topic/feed provisioning through DMAAP-BC, deployment possibly failed on the dependency. As these Bulk PM component/test are not modified with this change - dont see them as blocker.

As the other main components (ves/tca/prh/hv-ves) migrated are okay, good to proceed.

Patch Set 9:

> Patch Set 9: Code-Review-1
> 
> by the way, you've got helm hooks that may break OOM cleanup :(
> for example: https://gitlab.com/Orange-OpenSource/lfn/onap/onap_oom_automatic_installation/-/jobs/1087191331
> 
> please be sure your hook ends well when we're on corner case (database not there anymore especially)

I can't see exactly what's causing the problem in your cleanup case, but it's clear from the code that if Consul is not running at the time the hook executes, the hook will keep executing, waiting for Consul to come up.  (I missed this because I always use helm undeploy, which uninstalls in reverse alphabetical order, so that dcaegen2-services is always uninstalled before consul.)  I've raised an issue (https://jira.onap.org/browse/DCAEGEN2-2669) for this.

For the short term, I believe it's reasonable to simply remove the hook.  The hook is simply removing some configuration information from Consul.  It's harmless (at least for the 4 microservices we are
currently deploying), since a new deployment of a microservice after it has been deleted would simply overwrite any existing configuration.

Sorry... I was looking in the wrong place (of course the configMap is not changed!) and not thinking clearly about how/where the YAML is interpreted.
Two small issues, though:
-- The base64-encoded username:password string in AUTH_HDR can end in a "=", which means that the
   export can appear to be setting two different variables.  The AUTH_HDR ends up as "Basic".
-- In the tcagen2 values.yaml, the environment variable name AAI_USER did not agree with the
   name used in the application_config (AAI_USERNAME) so we ended up with a null in the configuration.
Patch coming after a bit more testing.

In the ONAP values.yaml, virtually everything is set to enabled:false by default.  The deployer overrides these values to select the parts of ONAP that they wish to deploy.   We do plan to use Helm-based deployment of these services in the H release.  If you look at the gating run outputs (for instance https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//118069-15/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/kubernetes-status/index.html), you can see that VES, HV-VES, TCA, and PRH have been deployed using Helm.
